title: Fundamentals of Mathematics
outline: The aim of this course is to familiarise students with the most fundamental concepts and tools of modern mathematics at an elementary level. Topics include: fundamentals of logic and set theory; concepts of a function; relations; equivalence and order relations; some basic mathematical structures; and the fundamental number systems. Students who intend to major in mathematics are expected to take this course during their first year.
lecture_times: Five lectures every two weeks in meridian.
dp_requirements: Minimum of 30% in year mark and satisfactory tutorial attendance.
assessment: Year mark counts 30–40%; one 2-hour examination written in June/July and one in October/November equally make up the balance.
